title: periploca apiculata
intro: Periploca apiculata, also known as poison vine or poison laurel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Periploca apiculata include its ability to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Periploca apiculata are typically made by infusing the leaves or roots in water, and it is also used in traditional remedies, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
